review_title: Powerful tool for managing development tasks and team collaboration
description: Overall, my experience with Jira has been very positive. It is a reliable platform for managing development tasks and keeping teams aligned on ongoing work. In our daily workflow, Jira helps us track bugs, manage feature development, and organize tasks across the team. Having everything documented in tickets makes it easier to follow progress and maintain transparency in the development process. Once you get familiar with the interface, it becomes a very effective tool for coordinating work and keeping projects organized.
author: Kaynan S.
pros: What I like most about Jira is how powerful it is for managing tasks and tracking development work. At NextDay, it helps organize issues, bugs, and feature requests in a structured way so the whole team can clearly see what needs to be done and what is currently in progress. The ability to create tickets, assign them to teammates, and track progress through boards makes collaboration much easier. I also like how flexible it is, since workflows, boards, and fields can be customized to match the team’s development process.
cons: One downside is that Jira can feel a bit complex, especially for new users who are not familiar with project management tools. There are many configuration options and features, which is powerful but can sometimes make the interface feel overwhelming at first. Some tasks that should be simple, like configuring workflows or searching for certain issues, can take a bit of time to learn.

review_title: Market standard ticketing platform
description: Been in 2 orgs so far and see ever using jira queues for tracking project timelines, tasks, sprints and individual work.
author: Satwik D.
pros: A complete ticketing system for tracking work, issues and team to work together. Market standard for tickets. Simple to get used to.
cons: Changes according to setup. poorly setup can be combersome for some orgs or teams. Should have a small learning curve.

review_title: Great software and loved using it.
description: Jira is good, but I had to leave it for Asana when my use case changed and found the latter is as useful and provides the integrations and features more suitable.
author: Samer A.
pros: It has a good value for the cost and provides high-quality services, and it is easy to use for updating tasks and scheduling work, besides having a good teamwork environment and collaborating on projects. In addition to that, it has many features to create boards, lists and diagrams.
cons: It is less colourful and simple that the other competition software, and the interface is so complex and crowded with options and settings.

review_title: Complete package for development team
description: Overall JiRa is one of the best tool for a team to manage a project as well as breaking down that project into doable tasks and then assign and distribute among the team.
author: Muhammad W.
pros: I love the UX of software that is easy to understand from a technical person to even a but leas technical people like project managers or product owners etc.
cons: The hierarchy of Jira is sometimes hard to understand like if there is user story and under that there are multiple cards ans so on. Sometimes if we try to complete a sprint its hard to tack down which one child task is not complete.

review_title: Jira project management
description: Very positive. I’ve used a number of project management tools and Jira is definitely the top choice for me.
author: Richard W.
pros: It has an intuitive interface, great for collaboration and one of the easier project management tools to use.
cons: There is definitely a learning curve to getting the most out of the platform. Interface can sometimes feel overwhelming.

review_title: Robust interface and variety of available tools
description: We are looking to transfer some of our functionality from ServiceNow to Jira and it seems to be working fine so far.
author: Kalpesh M.
pros: Its easy to use interface and available marketplace apps make integration easy and utilization at the highest level
cons: Support from the vendor can sometimes be challenging as we go through the changes that the enterprise requires

review_title: A Powerful Tool for Structured Support and Project Management
description: Overall, Jira has been a reliable and essential tool in my day‑to‑day work as a Technical Product Support Coordinator. It allows me to manage customer issues efficiently, collaborate with developers, and maintain clear audit trails for every task. Once configured properly, it significantly improves team coordination and transparency. Despite the complexity, the benefits outweigh the challenges, and it remains one of the best platforms for structured issue and project management.
author: Hadi B.
pros: Jira provides a highly adaptable and robust platform for handling technical workflows. Its customizable boards, automation features, and integration capabilities make it a great choice for bringing different teams together. From my perspective in product support, I’ve found it straightforward to follow issues from their first report all the way to resolution, always knowing who’s responsible and how things are progressing. The powerful search and filtering tools (JQL) are a huge help when managing lots of tickets.
cons: It can take a while to get used to Jira, especially if you’re new or need to dive into advanced setup and workflows. Some admin tasks feel more complicated than they should be, and the system can slow down if you’re working with big projects or lots of dashboard widgets. While the interface offers a lot, it can seem crowded at times, which can make it tougher for new or less technical team members to get started.

review_title: Great for dev teams, but can feel a bit heavy sometimes
description: The industry standard exists because it demonstrates its value. The system needs enhancements because its user interface operates at slow speeds although it successfully handles our daily standup meetings and sprint planning activities. The system functions as a reliable tool through its basic setup requirements which I use on a daily basis to handle my work and programming tasks.
author: Enguerran M.
pros: The best aspect of the software for developers exists in its ability to work together with Git and our CI/CD pipelines. The system provides a time-saving benefit because users can link Jira tickets to their work through pull requests and commit messages. The process for tracking sprints and transferring tickets throughout the board operates with high efficiency. The system enables our team to maintain shared understanding about which team members handle different bug fixes and user story tasks without requiring us to ask each other.
cons: The initial experience proves to be excessively challenging. The multiple menus and available options create a cumbersome experience when users attempt to perform basic task updates. The system becomes unmanageable when project managers and administrators create excessive custom workflows because users experience extreme difficulty in moving tickets to the "Done" status. The system experiences performance issues because it becomes slow and unresponsive when the backlog exceeds its capacity.

review_title: Industry Standard Work Management
description: Overall, my experience with Jira has been positive. It's a commonly used industry tool, and it is beneficial that most software engineers and product managers are familiar with using it.
author: Gunther C.
pros: Jira makes it easy to organize the work needed to complete projects. It's features help engineering leadership understand the status of in progress work, and it helps engineers distribute tasks amongst themselves.
cons: Jira is rather flexible, which is good, but this also makes it important for a company to set it up correctly so it is efficient and effective when engineers use it. Simple choices like disabling "initiative" level tickets can hinder the ability of teams.

review_title: Using JIRA to maximise team collaboration and track tasks
description: Generally it's quite positive. It allows easy tracking of progress on tasks and logging of time spent on said tasks.
author: Chaim S.
pros: the sprint kanban board makes it easy to track progress on tasks, it's easy to share progress on a ticket with other team members and to reassign tasks as necessary
cons: I find the UI to be quite unintuitive. It's also not particularly visually appealing. It has a feel of older generation software

review_title: Industry standard, good for dev teams but lacks flexibility for product work
description: Jira is powerful for engineering teams tracking tickets and sprints, but I find it rigid and poorly adapted to modern product workflows. The pricing structure is frustrating (you pay a lot to unlock the best features). Tools like Notion offer much more flexibility for how product teams actually work. While it remains the industry standard for dev teams, I now recommend more flexible alternatives for holistic product management.
author: Victor R.
pros: As a Head of Product with a PO/PM background, Jira excels at tracking technical work and sprint management. The integration with developer tools (Git, CI/CD) is solid. Backlog management and estimation features work well for traditional agile ceremonies. It's an industry standard, which means most tech teams are familiar with it. The reporting capabilities are comprehensive once you figure them out. For pure software development tracking, it does the job reliably.
cons: The tool feels rigid and doesn't adapt fluidly to diverse workflows. The pricing model is frustrating, you need to pay significantly more to unlock the most valuable features, which feels like nickel-and-diming. Compared to modern tools like Notion, Jira lacks flexibility and feels stuck in an older paradigm. The ticket-centric approach is limiting for product strategy work, discovery, and cross-functional collaboration. Customization is complex and requires admin expertise. The UX hasn't evolved much and feels dated compared to newer alternatives. It's built for engineering workflows but struggles with the broader product management needs like ideation, research synthesis, and stakeholder alignment.

review_title: Powerful Project Management with Advanced Workflow Capabilities
description: Jira is a powerful project management tool that scales well for teams of all sizes. While it requires some onboarding, the ability to track, prioritize, and report on tasks efficiently makes it invaluable for project-focused organizations.
author: Abraham E.
pros: Jira excels in project and task management, offering customizable workflows, robust reporting, and agile board functionality. It’s highly effective for team collaboration, keeping everyone aligned on deadlines and progress.
cons: The interface can feel overly complex for new users, and some features require a steep learning curve. Occasional performance lags occur with very large projects

review_title: A Powerful Tool That Brings Structure—But Can Feel Heavy at Times
description: Overall, my experience with Jira has been mostly positive. It’s a powerful tool for managing work and keeping teams aligned, especially on larger or more complex projects. While it can feel heavy and overly detailed at times, once you get used to it, it does a good job of bringing structure, visibility, and accountability to day-to-day work.
author: Leo D.
pros: What I liked most about Jira is that it keeps everyone on the same page. You can easily see who’s working on what, what’s blocked, and what needs attention next. It helps break big pieces of work into manageable tasks, keeps conversations and updates in one place, and reduces the need for constant follow-ups. Overall, it makes teamwork more organized and less chaotic.
cons: I didn’t like how complicated Jira can feel at times. For simple tasks, it often feels like overkill, with too many fields, steps, and settings. The interface can be cluttered, and a lot of time can end up going into managing tickets rather than actually getting work done.

review_title: Good tool for project tracking but has a learning curve
description: Overall my experience with Jira has been positive. It really helps in organizing work, tracking tasks and managing sprints in a structured way. Once you get familiar with how projects and workflows are set up, it becomes easier to use in day to day work. It improved visibility across the team and made collaboration smoother. There are few usability challenges but the benefits kind of outweigh the drawbacks for project tracking.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I liked most about Jira is how flexible it is for managing different kind of work. We used it for sprint planning, backlog tracking and day to day task updates, and it kept everything in one place which was really helpful. The boards (Scrum/Kanban) make it easy to see what everyone is working on and the progress of tickets without asking around. Also the integrations with other tools we use and the reporting dashboards are pretty useful, especially for tracking team performance and deadlines. It does take a little time to get used to, but once you start using it regularly it becomes part of the daily workflow.
cons: One thing I liked least about Jira is that it can feel a bit heavy and complex, especially for new users. There are so many fields, workflows and configurations that sometimes it becomes confusing to just create or update a simple ticket. Also performance can be slow at times, like pages take few extra seconds to load which gets frustrating when you are working on multiple tickets. The UI also feels cluttered in some areas and could be more simplified.

review_title: “A Robust Solution for Task and Incident Management”
description: My overall experience with Jira has been very positive. It has been a reliable tool for managing incidents, tracking tasks, and collaborating across teams. I found its ticketing system and customizable workflows especially useful for maintaining organization and visibility in day-to-day operations. While there is a bit of a learning curve initially, once familiar, it significantly improves efficiency and accountability. Overall, it’s a strong platform for teams that need structured tracking and effective project management.
author: Neha P.
pros: What I liked most about Jira is its flexibility and powerful issue-tracking capabilities. It makes it easy to create, prioritize, and track tickets across teams while maintaining full visibility of progress. The customizable workflows and dashboards help streamline processes and keep everyone aligned. I also found its integration with other tools and strong reporting features very useful for monitoring performance and improving team efficiency.
cons: What I liked least about Jira is that it can feel complex and overwhelming, especially for new users. The interface isn’t always intuitive, and navigating between boards, filters, and tickets can take time to get used to. Additionally, heavy customization can make workflows harder to manage, and performance may slow down when dealing with large volumes of tickets or data

review_title: Powerful but Complex Project Management for Serious Teams
description: My overall experience has been positive, especially once the system was tailored to my team’s workflow. It’s reliable, flexible, and extremely useful for keeping large or complex projects organized. However, the learning curve and setup time are significant, and smaller teams may find it more complicated than necessary. Once it’s configured well, Jira becomes a powerful central hub for project collaboration.
author: Wren W.
pros: I like that Jira gives teams a clear, detailed structure for managing work. It’s highly customizable, supports complex workflows, and makes it easy to track progress across sprints, epics, and projects. The integrations with Confluence, Git tools, and automation features are major advantages.
cons: Jira can quickly become overwhelming due to the number of menus, settings, and configuration options. It’s not very beginner-friendly, and building or adjusting workflows often requires admin help. The interface can feel cluttered, and simple tasks sometimes take more clicks than they should.

review_title: JIRA - It's What's For Dinner
description: It's been great - it's helped us reduce customer assistance times, and it also helps us keep track of our own progress internally, in a secure manner.
author: Drew L.
pros: The fact that it gives me the ability to manage all of my tickets, and ticketing options from a single pane of glass. This makes team collaboration and project collaboration a breeze. Being able to support customers is our main focus, and JIRA helps us do that very efficiently. It's easy to use, and its quite cheap. Gerat value for the money.
cons: It is very complex - there are a lot of bells and whistles and switches to toggle. You need to play around for a bit to get used to it.

review_title: A Powerful Tool for Managing Projects and Team Collaboration
description: My overall experience with Jira has been very positive. It keeps my entire team aligned and makes it easy to track progress, deadlines, and blockers. Once the workflows are set up properly, the productivity boost is huge. Automations help reduce manual work, and integrations make the system feel complete. It has definitely improved transparency and task ownership within our projects.
author: Jay G.
pros: Jira is extremely powerful for managing tasks, bugs, and project workflows. I really like its flexibility—whether it’s Scrum, Kanban, or a custom workflow, it can adapt to any team. The dashboard, automation rules, and integration with tools like Confluence, GitHub, and Slack make collaboration very smooth. Tracking sprint progress and issue history is also very easy. Overall, it brings great visibility and structure to daily work
cons: Jira can feel overwhelming for new users because of the number of features and configuration options. Some pages load a bit slow, and setting up custom workflows or permissions takes time. The UI has improved, but still can feel slightly complex for non-technical users.

review_title: The customer delight technology called JIRA.
description: My overall experience with Jira has been good because i was able to receive some training before i started using it.
author: Akintayo A.
pros: What i like most about Jira is that it connect all departments of an organization together in complaint resolution.
cons: What i dont like is that it cant not be used remotely or without internet .The interphase is not also user friendly.

review_title: Great product once you get used to it
description: Overall, my experience was excellent. The majority of the time I was working with Epix, which allowed me to create tickets and point and color code and create an easy view for my team. It was difficult at times when I was looking for historical data and metrics for each Engineer. You have to learn its own clearing language, but it wasn’t too bad.
author: Ally M.
pros: I genuinely appreciated the work that went in to making project boards understandable and easy to work with. I could color code and see the scope of work for each engineer and for each project.
cons: Querying can be difficult and often I wasn’t seeing all of the historical work for an engineer that I thought I should be.

review_title: Great tool for keeping on top of projects
description: It is a great tool for managing projects, reports help keep projects on track and reduce end of month admin
author: Michael M.
pros: We use Jira to track project bugs and time management of our team. Best ability is being able to generate reports and see where we need to improve are process on projects
cons: The interface, it is a little bit all over the place. Switching between desktop and mobile some required fields are hidden

review_title: The Most Robust Project Portfolio Management that Ever Existed
description: I use Jira to track quality improvement initiatives in my hospital to help improve customer satisfaction.
author: Amber L.
pros: Jira is highly customizable which makes it easy to tailor to our project and task management needs. It has a mobile application for iOS system that has a clean UI and UX than any other tool that I use for PPM.
cons: I am able to acquire team and project management needs without facing issues when using Jira in the healthcare industry.

review_title: Fantabulous PPM of 2025 that I Highly Trust
author: Darien B.
pros: There are tons of PPM tools in the market, but Jira is my favorite. I like that it allocate robust team collaboration, project coordination and task management tools unlike any other alternative that I am currently using.
cons: Jira for over six years had been my go-to project portfolio management program - the new features that are in the new version 237 are not complicated to customize since the vendor offered free e-books and tutorials.

review_title: Powerful, Painful, but Practically Unavoidable
description: Powerful for tracking work, but clunky UI, heavy setup, and slow workflows make daily use frustrating.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Customizable workflows, powerful issue tracking, strong integrations, granular permissions, and clear visibility into team progress made Jira effective for managing complex projects at scale.
cons: Cluttered UI, steep learning curve, and too many settings made simple tasks feel heavier and slower than necessary.

review_title: A comprehensive solution for Agile teams
description: Jira is an incredibly reliable platform that scales perfectly with the size of a project. I've found it to be the best tool for keeping a clear backlog and managing sprints. It isn't always the fastest or most intuitive interface, but once you have your workflows dialed in, it’s a powerhouse for productivity. It's my go-to for technical project management.
author: Camilo I.
pros: What I like most is how perfectly it supports Agile methodologies. The Scrum and Kanban boards are incredibly detailed, and features like Backlog grooming and Sprint planning are built-in natively. It makes managing a complex development lifecycle much more structured than simpler task managers.
cons: At times, the interface can feel a bit sluggish, especially when dealing with large backlogs or complex projects with many custom fields. I’ve noticed that loading times for boards and reports can be a little slow, which can be frustrating when you’re trying to make quick updates during a meeting.
